---
title: "End of Day 1"
subtitle: "Recap and Preview"
day: "Day 1"
---

## What we covered today

::: incremental
- **Writing in Quarto** — Markdown, citations, cross-references, numbered examples
- **Output formats** — HTML, Word, PDF, `keep-tex`, APA 7 trade-offs
- **Data in Quarto** — code chunks, chunk options, loading data, inline reporting
:::


::: {style="text-align: center; margin-top: 1em; color: white;"}
### 💬 Reflect {background-color="#8D203D"}

*What was the most useful thing you learned today?*

. . .

*What are you still unsure about?*

. . .

*Did anything surprise you?*

. . .

*~3 minutes*
:::

## Before Day 2

::: incremental
- Make sure your RProject is set up and your data is in `data/raw/`
- Try rendering `exercises/day1.qmd` to all three formats if you haven't already
- Optional: have a look at your own analysis script; where could inline reporting replace manual transcription?
:::


## Day 2 preview

::: incremental
- **Open and reproducible research** — FAIR principles, data life cycle, modular analyses
- **Model results** — `broom.mixed`, `modelsummary`, inline reporting
- **Summary tables** — `gt`, `kableExtra`, `tribble`, format-conditional output
- **Figures** — `ggplot2`, exporting, cross-referencing
:::

::: {style="text-align: center; margin-top: 2em; color: white;"}
### See you next month {background-color="#8D203D"}

Any questions before we finish?

. . .

*Thank you for your attention and engagement today.*
:::
